d31c12ae21bcc097e65698b189841619e20957d8: {"teaching_note": "Predicting SalePrice means fitting a line (well, a many-dimensional version of one) that turns a house's measurable traits — its size, quality, age, and location — into a best-guess dollar figure, the same way simple linear regression draws the straight line through a scatter of points. Each feature you pick gets its own weight, and the model learns how many dollars a bump in that feature tends to add or subtract from the price.", "split_note": "You're choosing to set aside a random one-fifth of these 1,460 houses and hide their real prices while the model learns from the other four-fifths. Then you check the model's guesses against those held-out prices — an honesty test that shows whether it truly learned what drives price or just memorized the houses it already saw.", "features": [{"name": "OverallQual", "reason": "A 1-to-10 rating of overall material and finish quality, which is usually the single strongest signal of how much a house is worth."}, {"name": "GrLivArea", "reason": "The above-ground living area in square feet, and bigger living space almost always commands a higher price."}, {"name": "TotalBsmtSF", "reason": "Total basement square footage adds usable space that buyers pay extra for."}, {"name": "YearBuilt", "reason": "The construction year captures how new a house is, and newer homes tend to sell for more."}, {"name": "YearRemodAdd", "reason": "The year of the last remodel signals how updated the home feels, which lifts its price."}, {"name": "Neighborhood", "reason": "The named location is a classic price driver because identical houses cost different amounts in different areas."}, {"name": "LotArea", "reason": "The lot size in square feet reflects how much land comes with the house, a direct part of its value."}, {"name": "GarageCars", "reason": "How many cars the garage holds is a simple, meaningful gauge of garage value to buyers."}, {"name": "GarageArea", "reason": "The garage's square footage adds detail about parking and storage space beyond just the car count."}, {"name": "FullBath", "reason": "The number of full bathrooms above grade is a well-known factor in how buyers judge a home's worth."}, {"name": "1stFlrSF", "reason": "First-floor square footage measures the main living level that shapes a home's footprint and price."}, {"name": "TotRmsAbvGrd", "reason": "The total count of above-grade rooms summarizes how much livable room the house offers."}, {"name": "Fireplaces", "reason": "The number of fireplaces is a desirable amenity that nudges the price upward."}, {"name": "KitchenQual", "reason": "A quality rating for the kitchen matters because kitchens strongly influence what buyers will pay."}, {"name": "MasVnrArea", "reason": "The masonry veneer area reflects a premium exterior finish that can raise perceived value."}, {"name": "OverallCond", "reason": "A 1-to-9 rating of the home's overall condition tells the model how well-maintained the house is."}]}
35e191cad79f00fddef1a9197d1db8f294ab1bfb: {"teaching_note": "Predicting SalePrice means fitting a line (well, a many-dimensional version of one) where each house feature nudges the estimated dollar price up or down, just like a simple regression draws the best straight line through points. Once fit, the model reads a new home's characteristics and adds up those nudges to guess what it should sell for.", "split_note": "You're choosing to hide a random fifth of the 1,460 homes from the model while it learns, then quiz it on those unseen houses — an honesty test that shows whether it truly learned price patterns or just memorized the homes it saw.", "features": [{"name": "OverallQual", "reason": "An overall quality rating from 1 to 10 that captures how well-built a home is, usually the strongest single driver of price."}, {"name": "GrLivArea", "reason": "The above-ground living area in square feet, since bigger livable space typically commands a higher price."}, {"name": "TotalBsmtSF", "reason": "The total basement square footage, adding usable space that buyers pay extra for."}, {"name": "GarageCars", "reason": "How many cars the garage holds, a simple stand-in for garage size and convenience that buyers value."}, {"name": "GarageArea", "reason": "The garage's square footage, giving a finer measure of parking and storage space than car count alone."}, {"name": "YearBuilt", "reason": "The construction year, since newer homes generally sell for more than older ones."}, {"name": "YearRemodAdd", "reason": "The year of the last remodel, because recent updates tend to lift a home's value."}, {"name": "FullBath", "reason": "The number of full bathrooms, a feature buyers weigh heavily when comparing homes."}, {"name": "Neighborhood", "reason": "The physical location within Ames, which strongly shapes price through desirability and local demand."}, {"name": "1stFlrSF", "reason": "The first-floor square footage, another direct measure of a home's size and footprint."}, {"name": "LotArea", "reason": "The lot size in square feet, since more land usually adds to a property's worth."}, {"name": "TotRmsAbvGrd", "reason": "The count of rooms above ground, reflecting overall spaciousness that affects price."}, {"name": "Fireplaces", "reason": "The number of fireplaces, a desirable comfort feature that can raise a home's appeal and price."}, {"name": "KitchenQual", "reason": "The kitchen quality rating, because a nicer kitchen is a well-known selling point."}, {"name": "ExterQual", "reason": "The quality of the exterior material, signaling curb appeal and build quality that influence value."}, {"name": "MasVnrArea", "reason": "The masonry veneer area in square feet, an upgrade finish that tends to nudge price upward."}, {"name": "BsmtFinSF1", "reason": "The square footage of finished basement space, which is more valuable than unfinished area."}, {"name": "CentralAir", "reason": "Whether the home has central air conditioning, a comfort amenity buyers are willing to pay for."}]}

6589ff85945c9fa4833e33095c04cfea1228f7e0: {"teaching_note": "Predicting SalePrice means fitting a line (really a weighted sum) that estimates a home's dollar value from its measurable traits, so each feature gets a coefficient describing how much price moves when that trait changes. Just like basic linear regression drawing a best-fit line through points, we're finding the combination of size, quality, and location that best tracks what buyers actually paid.", "split_note": "You're choosing to set aside a random fifth of the 1,460 homes before training and never let the model see their prices while it learns. That held-out group becomes your honesty test: if the model predicts their prices well too, you can trust it learned real patterns rather than memorizing the homes it studied.", "features": [{"name": "OverallQual", "reason": "The 1-to-10 rating of overall material and finish quality is usually the single strongest signal of how much a home is worth."}, {"name": "GrLivArea", "reason": "Above-grade living area in square feet captures how much house a buyer gets, which strongly drives price."}, {"name": "Neighborhood", "reason": "Which named Ames neighborhood a home sits in reflects location desirability, and location is a classic price driver."}, {"name": "YearBuilt", "reason": "The original construction year proxies for age, style, and wear, with newer homes generally commanding more."}, {"name": "YearRemodAdd", "reason": "The remodel or addition year signals recent updates, and a freshly modernized home tends to sell for more."}, {"name": "TotalBsmtSF", "reason": "Total basement square footage adds usable space that buyers pay for beyond the main floors."}, {"name": "1stFlrSF", "reason": "First-floor area measures the home's footprint, a concrete size input closely tied to value."}, {"name": "FullBath", "reason": "The number of above-grade full bathrooms is a well-known convenience feature that lifts sale prices."}, {"name": "GarageCars", "reason": "Garage capacity in cars reflects parking and storage, a practical amenity buyers reward."}, {"name": "GarageArea", "reason": "Garage size in square feet gives a finer measure of that same valued parking and storage space."}, {"name": "LotArea", "reason": "Total lot size in square feet captures land value, which adds to a property's overall worth."}, {"name": "ExterQual", "reason": "The quality rating of the exterior material signals curb appeal and build standard that influence price."}, {"name": "KitchenQual", "reason": "Kitchen quality is a room buyers weigh heavily, so higher ratings typically push the price up."}, {"name": "BsmtQual", "reason": "Basement quality, mainly ceiling height, indicates how livable that lower space is and thus its added value."}, {"name": "Fireplaces", "reason": "The count of fireplaces is a desirable comfort feature that tends to nudge sale prices higher."}, {"name": "CentralAir", "reason": "Whether the home has central air conditioning is a comfort amenity many buyers pay a premium for."}]}
